summarization.a: Figures show schematic of the pulsing sequence of the ALD cycle for depositions of Li2CO3 by thermal ALD process
summarization.b: Schematic of plasma-assisted ALD of Li2CO3

a.question: What is H2O reaction?
a.answer:
The H2O dose during the thermal ALD process was only 50 ms, after which all valves were closed for 4 s to allow for the molecules to react. Therefore, this is
referred to as “H2O reaction”.

a.question: Describe the thermal ALD cycle
a.answer:
For thermal ALD, the cycle consists of 
1. the exposure of the substrate to LiOtBu (97%, Sigma Aldrich) lithium precursor
2.  H2O gas 
3. CO2 gas 
all alternated by argon purges

b.question: Are different lithium precursors used for thermal and plasma ALD?
How is plasma ALD executed?
b.answer:
The plasma-assisted ALD process consists of a combination of the substrate exposure to the same lithium precursor and to an O2-fed plasma with a plasma power of 100 W, alternated by argon purging steps. To ensure the gas
flow and pressure have stabilized, the reactor was  filed with oxygen prior to switching on the plasma source
